steps.title: Boric acid buffer Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Boric acid buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Boric acid 50 mM 1.24 g Sodium tetraborate 12 mM 1.90 g Adjust to pH 8.5 with 1 M NaOH, add sterile ddH 2 O to 400 mL, and store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Poly-L-lysine stock solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Poly-L-lysine stock solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Poly-L-lysine 1 mg/mL 50 mg Boric acid buffer 50 mL Use the top bottle filter to sterilize and store at 4 °C. The working solution is made by diluting the stock solution with boric acid buffer to 100 μg/mL.

steps.title: Calcium- and magnesium-free HBSS (CMF-HBSS) Reagent...
steps.instruction: Calcium- and magnesium-free HBSS (CMF-HBSS) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 1 M HEPES 10 mM 5 mL Penicillin–streptomycin (10,000 U/mL) 100 U/mL 5 mL 10× HBSS 1× 50 mL Add sterile ddH 2 O to 500 mL and store at 4 °C.

steps.title: DNase I solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: DNase I solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume DNase I 10 mg/mL 10 mg ddH 2 O 1 mL Aliquot into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes and store at -20 °C.

steps.title: Digestion medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Digestion medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 1 M HEPES 10 mM 1 mL 0.25% trypsin-EDTA 100 mL Aliquot into 15 mL centrifuge tubes and store at -20 °C.

steps.title: L-glutamine solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: L-glutamine solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume L-glutamine 200 mM 1.46 g Add ddH 2 O to 50 mL. Warm up in a 37 °C water bath for 30 min, vortex the tube every 5 min to ensure L-glutamine is dissolved, aliquot into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes, and store at -20 °C.

steps.title: Neuronal plating medium Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Neuronal plating medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume D-glucose 0.6% 200 μL FBS 5% 750 μL L-glutamine solution 2 mM 150 μL Add MEM so the final volume is 15 mL.

steps.title: Neuronal maintenance medium Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Neuronal maintenance medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume L-glutamine solution (200 mM) 0.5 mM 125 μL B27 supplement 50× 1× 1 mL Add neurobasal medium so the final volume is 50 mL. B27 is light-sensitive. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: 50 mL centrifuge tube (Corning, catalog number:
steps.instruction: 50 mL centrifuge tube (Corning, catalog number:

steps.instruction: 15 mL centrifuge tube (Corning, catalog number:

steps.title: 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (SSI Bio, catalog number
steps.instruction: 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (SSI Bio, catalog number: 1SSI-1260-00)

steps.title: Wash 12 mm coverslips once with...
steps.instruction: Wash 12 mm coverslips once with acetone and twice with absolute ethanol on a shaker (90 rpm) for 15 min at room temperature.

steps.title: After the second wash with ethanol,...
steps.instruction: After the second wash with ethanol, transfer coverslips into the laminar flow cabinet and then wash with sterile ddH 2 O thrice.

steps.title: Transfer coverslips into each well of a 24-well plate
steps.instruction: Transfer coverslips into each well of a 24-well plate. Remove ddH 2 O using aspiration. For 24-well plates, apply 200 μL of 100 μg/mL poly-L-lysine (PLL) solution to each well and incubate overnight at room temperature for coverslip coating. For 10 cm cell culture dishes, add 7 mL of 100 μg/mL PLL solution. Avoid UV light exposure after PLL was applied.

steps.title: After PLL coating, wash coverslips with...
steps.instruction: After PLL coating, wash coverslips with ddH 2 O twice and air dry.

steps.title: PLL-coated coverslips or dishes can be...
steps.instruction: PLL-coated coverslips or dishes can be kept in the refrigerator for up to one week. B. Neuron preparation and culture for primary hippocampal and cortical neurons

steps.title: Euthanize pregnant E17
steps.instruction: Euthanize pregnant E17.5 C57BL/6NCrlBltw dams with CO 2 .

steps.title: Sterilize the abdominal fur by spraying with 75% ethanol
steps.instruction: Sterilize the abdominal fur by spraying with 75% ethanol.

steps.title: Cut the fur, exposing the skin and muscle layer
steps.instruction: Cut the fur, exposing the skin and muscle layer.

steps.title: Isolate the uterus with embryos from...
steps.instruction: Isolate the uterus with embryos from the abdominal cavity using the Dumont #5 forceps.

steps.title: Take out embryos from the uterus...
steps.instruction: Take out embryos from the uterus using Dumont #5 forceps and remove the placentas. Keep in ice-cold CMF-HBSS in a Petri dish before the brain dissection.

steps.title: Hold the E17
steps.instruction: Hold the E17.5 embryos by the neck with Dumont #5 forceps and use the tip of the Dumont #7 forceps to gently cut open the skin and the skull. Carefully cut open the skull from the nose to the back of the head and squeeze out the brain by pinching the sides of the skull.

steps.title: Place the embryonic brains in a...
steps.instruction: Place the embryonic brains in a new Petri dish filled with ice-cold CMF-HBSS.

steps.title: Operating under a stereomicroscope, separate brain...
steps.instruction: Operating under a stereomicroscope, separate brain cortexes from each brain and remove the meninges using a pair of Dumont #5 forceps.

steps.title: Dissect hippocampi from both cortical hemispheres...
steps.instruction: Dissect hippocampi from both cortical hemispheres and remove the fimbriae on the concave side using the Dumont #5 forceps and the iridectomy scissors.

steps.title: Transfer hippocampi and cortexes using Dumont...
steps.instruction: Transfer hippocampi and cortexes using Dumont #7 forceps into two different 15 mL centrifuge tubes containing 5 mL of digestion medium and incubate at 37 °C for 30 min.

steps.title: Replace the digestion medium with room-temperature...
steps.instruction: Replace the digestion medium with room-temperature CMF-HBSS and let it stand for 3 min. Repeat the step twice to allow the residual digestion medium to diffuse from the tissue.

steps.title: Apply 100 μL of DNase I...
steps.instruction: Apply 100 μL of DNase I solution to the hippocampi-containing CMF-HBSS and 200 μL of DNase I solution to the cortex-containing CMF-HBSS.

steps.title: Then, triturate hippocampi 10 times with...
steps.instruction: Then, triturate hippocampi 10 times with a 10 mL serological pipette and 10 times with a 10 mL serological pipette tipped with a 1,000 μL tip until the tissue dissociates. Triturate cortexes 20 times with a 10 mL serological pipette and 20 times with a 10 mL serological pipette tipped with a 1,000 μL tip until the tissue dissociates. Then, filter the tissue through the 70 μm cell strainer to remove tissue debris.

steps.title: Centrifuge dissociated neurons at 100× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge dissociated neurons at 100× g for 10 min at room temperature and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Dissociate the cell pellet by flicking...
steps.instruction: Dissociate the cell pellet by flicking the bottom of the microcentrifuge tube a few times and resuspending in the 37 °C prewarmed and pre-equilibrated neuronal plating medium.

steps.title: Determine cell number using a hemocytometer...
steps.instruction: Determine cell number using a hemocytometer in the presence of 0.2% trypan blue solution.

steps.title: Seed dissociated neurons onto poly-L-lysine-coated coverslips (2
steps.instruction: Seed dissociated neurons onto poly-L-lysine-coated coverslips (2.5 × 10 3 cells/cm 2 for low-density cultures and 3 × 10 4 cells/cm 2 for regular-density cultures) in 500 μL of prewarmed and pre-equilibrated neuronal plating medium.

steps.title: After 4 hours, replace the neuronal...
steps.instruction: After 4 hours, replace the neuronal plating medium with the prewarmed and pre-equilibrated 50% freshly made neuronal plating medium mixed with 50% conditioned medium (for low-density cultures) or neuronal maintenance medium (for regular-density cultures). To prepare the conditioned medium, seed 2.2 × 10 6 cortical neurons in a poly-L-lysine-coated 10 cm cell culture dish and culture for 7 days. The cell culture medium is the conditioned medium; it can be stored at -20 °C for several months. C. Electroporation

steps.title: Centrifuge 1 × 10 6 dissociated...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 1 × 10 6 dissociated neurons at 80× g for 10 min at room temperature and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Carefully resuspend the neuron pellet in...
steps.instruction: Carefully resuspend the neuron pellet in 100 μL of room-temperature mouse neuron nucleofector solution plus 3 μg of DNA. Then, transfer the mixture into a sterile cuvette.

steps.title: Perform electroporation with the Nucleofector Program...
steps.instruction: Perform electroporation with the Nucleofector Program O-005 for mouse neuron nucleofector.

steps.title: Add 330 μL of prewarmed and...
steps.instruction: Add 330 μL of prewarmed and pre-equilibrated RPMI 1640 medium to a cuvette immediately after electroporation and transfer gently (without pipetting up and down) into a 1.5 mL tube for recovery (incubating for 10 min in a 37 °C CO 2 incubator).

steps.title: After the recovery step, seed neurons...
steps.instruction: After the recovery step, seed neurons into each well containing the prewarmed and pre-equilibrated neuronal plating medium using the aforementioned density.

steps.title: After 4 h, replace the neuronal...
steps.instruction: After 4 h, replace the neuronal plating medium with the fresh neuronal maintenance medium.

steps.title: The protein expressed from the plasmid...
steps.instruction: The protein expressed from the plasmid can usually be detected in 24 h. D. Cationic lipid transfection

steps.title: Transfect neurons in each well at...
steps.instruction: Transfect neurons in each well at least 48 h after seeding using Lipofectamine 2000 (LFA). In our experience, transfecting neurons 48 h after seeding causes severe cell death.

steps.title: For one reaction, dilute 1 μg...
steps.instruction: For one reaction, dilute 1 μg of plasmid DNA and 1 μL of LFA with 50 μL each of neurobasal medium (NB) to make the DNA/NB complex solution and the LFA/NB complex solution. This plasmid DNA-to-LFA ratio must be empirically determined for optimal transfection efficiency.

steps.title: After 5 min, pipette the DNA/NB...
steps.instruction: After 5 min, pipette the DNA/NB complex solution into the LFA/NB solution. Mix the mixture by flicking the bottom of the tube and leave to stand at room temperature for 20 min before adding it to each neuron-containing well.

steps.title: Forty-five minutes after transfection, replace the...
steps.instruction: Forty-five minutes after transfection, replace the medium with prewarmed and pre-equilibrated 50% freshly made neuronal plating medium mixed with 50% conditioned medium (for low-density cultures) or neuronal maintenance medium (for regular-density cultures).

steps.title: The protein expressed from the plasmid...
steps.instruction: The protein expressed from the plasmid can usually be detected in 24 h.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it have been used and validated in the following research article(s): • Chen et al. [11]. The non-mitotic role of HMMR in regulating the localization of TPX2 and the dynamics of microtubules in neurons. eLife 13. https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.94547
